tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use a hybrid approach: they run behavioral simulations via subprocess (executing Python code that demonstrates the bug pattern and fix) AND perform AST structural verification. They don't just grep for strings in files — they parse ASTs and execute behavioral code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests include structural AST checks that verify specific code patterns in the actual source files. A stub/no-op implementation would fail because the base commit code has the buggy patterns (e.g., use_rocm_aiter in the condition gate, no refresh_env_variables call, direct dict mutation). The tests check the actual repo files, not just the simulation.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh file exists in the container at /workspace and contains the exact gold patch. An agent could read it directly (cat /workspace/vllm/../../solution/solve.sh or similar path traversal). The Dockerfile doesn't show any step removing the solution directory, and the tests run in the same container.
